title: cymbopogon nardus var. luridus
intro: Cymbopogon nardus var. luridus, also known as East Indian lemongrass, is a variant of the cymbopogon nardus plant, a species of grass native to tropical and subtropical regions. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, attributed to its high content of citral and other volatile oils. The health benefits associated with East Indian lemongrass include its potential to reduce stress and anxiety, alleviate pain and inflammation, and aid in digestion, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. East Indian lemongrass can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, essential oils, and infusions, which can be consumed or applied topically to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
